message:
feat: Add automatic cache cleanup with 30-day retention

This implements smart cache management to prevent unbounded disk growth
while maintaining performance benefits.

## Cache Retention Policy
- **30-day default retention** for system prompts and conversation logs
- Optimization profiles and shaders kept forever (small, model-specific)
- Automatic cleanup during `zell build`
- Manual cleanup via enhanced `zell clear-cache` command

## Implementation Details

### snapshot.zig
- Added `cleanupOldSystemPromptCaches()` - removes caches >30 days
- Added `cleanupOldConversationLogs()` - removes logs >30 days
- Added `CACHE_RETENTION_DAYS` constant (configurable)
- Uses file mtime to determine age

### main.zig
- Auto-cleanup during `zell build` (silent, non-blocking)
- Enhanced `zell clear-cache` with options:
  - `zell clear-cache` - clean old only (default, safe)
  - `zell clear-cache --old` - explicit old cleanup
  - `zell clear-cache --all` - nuclear option
  - `zell clear-cache <model>` - specific model only

### CACHE.md
- Comprehensive cache management documentation
- Explains cache types, sizes, retention policy
- Troubleshooting guide
- Performance impact analysis

## User Experience

### Automatic (Zero Config)
```bash
zell build model.gguf
# Output includes:
# 🧹 Cleaned up 5 old cache(s) and 12 conversation log(s)
```

### Manual Cleanup
```bash
# Safe: clean only old caches (>30 days)
zell clear-cache

# Nuclear: clear everything
zell clear-cache --all

# Specific model only
zell clear-cache model.gguf
```

## Cache Invalidation
- System prompts: automatic via content hash
- Different prompt → different hash → different cache file
- Old caches cleaned up automatically after 30 days
- No manual invalidation needed!

## Disk Space Impact
- Typical cache size: 20-150MB
- After cleanup: removes unused caches
- Retains recent caches for 36.9% speedup

This ensures Zell stays fast while being a good citizen with disk space.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,258 @@
+# Cache Management
+
+Zell uses several caches to improve performance. This document explains how caches work and how to manage them.
+
+## Cache Types
+
+### 1. System Prompt Caches (`~/.cache/zell/system-prompts/`)
+
+**Purpose:** Stores KV cache state for system prompts to enable instant startup
+
+**How it works:**
+- Each unique system prompt gets a hash-based cache file (e.g., `068edcf4b39661f7.kv`)
+- When you use the same system prompt again, Zell loads the cached KV state instead of reprocessing
+- **Performance impact:** 36.9% faster inference (1.58x speedup)
+
+**Size:** ~600KB - 1.3MB per system prompt
+
+**Example:**
+```bash
+# First time: processes system prompt (~200ms)
+zell build model.gguf --system-prompt "You are a Zig expert."
+
+# Subsequent runs: instant restore (~0ms)
+zell run model.gguf "You are a Zig expert.\n\nWrite hello world"
+# ✓ Found cached system prompt (instant restore)
+```
+
+### 2. Conversation Logs (`~/.cache/zell/conversations/{model_hash}/`)
+
+**Purpose:** Automatically logs all conversations for background LoRA fine-tuning
+
+**How it works:**
+- Every conversation is logged to JSONL files organized by date
+- After 100 conversations, triggers automatic background LoRA training
+- Trained adapters are stored in `~/.cache/zell/lora/{model_hash}_auto.lora`
+
+**Size:** Varies (each conversation ~1-10KB in JSONL format)
+
+**Example directory:**
+```
+~/.cache/zell/conversations/a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8/
+├── 19723.jsonl  # Dec 1, 2025
+├── 19724.jsonl  # Dec 2, 2025
+└── 19725.jsonl  # Dec 3, 2025
+```
+
+### 3. Optimization Profiles (`~/.cache/zell/profiles/`)
+
+**Purpose:** Stores model analysis results from `zell build`
+
+**How it works:**
+- Analyzes model architecture, memory requirements, optimal GPU/CPU split
+- Cached after first `zell build` command
+- Reused on every subsequent run
+
+**Size:** ~1-5KB per model (JSON)
+
+### 4. Metal Shaders (`~/.cache/zell/shaders/`)
+
+**Purpose:** Pre-compiled GPU shaders for macOS Metal acceleration
+
+**How it works:**
+- Compiled during `zell build`
+- Dramatically reduces first-run latency on macOS
+
+**Size:** Varies by model
+
+---
+
+## Cache Retention Policy
+
+**Default retention:** 30 days
+
+Zell automatically cleans up old caches to prevent unbounded disk usage while keeping recent caches for performance.
+
+### What Gets Cleaned Up
+
+- **System prompt caches** older than 30 days
+- **Conversation logs** older than 30 days
+- **NOT cleaned:** Optimization profiles (small, model-specific)
+- **NOT cleaned:** Metal shaders (small, model-specific)
+
+### When Cleanup Happens
+
+**Automatic cleanup:**
+- Every time you run `zell build`
+- Silently removes old files in the background
+
+**Manual cleanup:**
+```bash
+# Clean old caches only (>30 days) - SAFE, recommended
+zell clear-cache
+# or explicitly:
+zell clear-cache --old
+
+# Clean ALL caches (including recent ones) - CAUTION
+zell clear-cache --all
+
+# Clean caches for specific model only
+zell clear-cache model.gguf
+```
+
+---
+
+## Cache Invalidation
+
+### System Prompt Caches
+
+**Automatic invalidation:** Caches are content-addressed by hash
+
+If you change your system prompt, Zell automatically:
+1. Generates a new hash from the new prompt text
+2. Looks for a cache file matching the new hash
+3. If not found, processes the new prompt and caches it
+4. Old cache file remains but is unused (cleaned up after 30 days)
+
+**Example:**
+```bash
+# Build with prompt A
+zell build model.gguf --system-prompt "You are a helpful assistant."
+# Creates: ~/.cache/zell/system-prompts/a1b2c3d4.kv
+
+# Change to prompt B
+zell build model.gguf --system-prompt "You are a Rust expert."
+# Creates: ~/.cache/zell/system-prompts/9876fedc.kv
+
+# Both caches coexist, old one cleaned after 30 days
+```
+
+### Optimization Profiles
+
+**Manual invalidation required:**
+```bash
+# If model file changes or you want to rebuild:
+zell build model.gguf --force
+
+# Or clear cache and rebuild:
+zell clear-cache model.gguf
+zell build model.gguf
+```
+
+---
+
+## Disk Space Usage
+
+Typical cache sizes:
+
+| Cache Type | Size per Item | Retention | Total (estimated) |
+|------------|---------------|-----------|-------------------|
+| System prompts | 600KB - 1.3MB | 30 days | ~10-50MB |
+| Conversations | 1-10KB/conv | 30 days | ~1-10MB |
+| Optimization profiles | 1-5KB | Forever | <1MB |
+| Metal shaders | Varies | Forever | ~10-100MB |
+
+**Total cache size:** Typically 20-150MB depending on usage
+
+---
+
+## Configuration
+
+### Changing Retention Period
+
+The retention period is configurable in `src/snapshot.zig`:
+
+```zig
+/// Cache retention policy (in seconds)
+pub const CACHE_RETENTION_DAYS = 30;  // Change this value
+pub const CACHE_RETENTION_SECONDS = CACHE_RETENTION_DAYS * 24 * 60 * 60;
+```
+
+To change:
+1. Edit `src/snapshot.zig` and modify `CACHE_RETENTION_DAYS`
+2. Rebuild: `zig build`
+3. Future cleanup operations will use the new retention period
+
+---
+
+## Troubleshooting
+
+### "Cache taking up too much space"
+
+```bash
+# Check cache sizes
+du -sh ~/.cache/zell/*
+
+# Clean old caches
+zell clear-cache --old
+
+# Nuclear option: clear everything
+zell clear-cache --all
+```
+
+### "System prompt cache not loading"
+
+Check if cache exists:
+```bash
+ls -lh ~/.cache/zell/system-prompts/
+
+# If corrupted, rebuild:
+zell clear-cache --all
+zell build model.gguf --system-prompt "Your prompt"
+```
+
+### "Want to disable conversation logging"
+
+Currently not configurable. Conversation logging is a core feature for automatic long-term memory.
+
+To manually delete conversation logs:
+```bash
+rm -rf ~/.cache/zell/conversations/
+```
+
+---
+
+## Performance Impact
+
+### With Caching (Recommended)
+```
+Cold start:  2,949ms
+Warm start:  1,861ms (36.9% faster)
+Speedup:     1.58x
+```
+
+### Without Caching (Not Recommended)
+```
+Every run:   2,949ms
+No speedup
+```
+
+**Recommendation:** Keep caching enabled. The disk space cost (20-150MB) is negligible compared to the 36.9% performance improvement.
+
+---
+
+## Cache Locations
+
+All Zell caches are stored under `~/.cache/zell/`:
+
+```
+~/.cache/zell/
+├── system-prompts/          # System prompt KV caches
+│   ├── 068edcf4b39661f7.kv
+│   └── 76b6428bafb1e461.kv
+├── conversations/           # Conversation logs (per model)
+│   └── {model_hash}/
+│       ├── 19723.jsonl
+│       └── 19724.jsonl
+├── lora/                    # Auto-trained LoRA adapters
+│   └── {model_hash}_auto.lora
+├── profiles/                # Model optimization profiles
+│   └── model.gguf.json
+└── shaders/                 # Pre-compiled Metal shaders
+    └── model.gguf.metallib
+```
+
+To completely remove all Zell data:
+```bash
+rm -rf ~/.cache/zell/
+```
